holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: No dated company catalyst is on the board for INTC, so today's -5% on thin volume (0.31 RV) is noise to respect, not act on — the 5d is still +4.8%, meaning you're holding through chop, not a trend break. With the next real event being Q3 earnings (late-Oct, not yet on the supplied calendar) and triple witching ~87 days out, the window to lean in either direction hasn't opened yet. Let price confirm whether today's air-pocket fills back or extends before adding to an 8-share starter.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro, only dated event supplied)
INTC.bull: 5d still green despite today's drop and low RV suggests forced/illiquid selling, not fundamental deterioration — base case for a bounce if no news emerges.
INTC.bear: No confirmed earnings date on the sheet means you're flying blind into the next print, and a -5% day on no news can be the first tell of something not yet public.

NVDA.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for NVDA right now, and the tape is doing the work for you: -3% today, -4.8% on the week suggests positioning is unwinding into a vacuum. With nothing dated to anchor an add, the honest move is to let the selloff find a level and wait for either the next earnings setup to come into view or for price to stop going down. The Sept 18 triple witching is a flow event, not a fundamental one — useful for timing volatility, not for sizing conviction.
NVDA.bull: Quality AI compounder pulling back into a no-news window — weakness here is the setup if it stabilizes ahead of the next print.
NVDA.bear: No dated company catalyst to backstop the drawdown; -4.8% in 5 days can extend if AI-trade positioning keeps unwinding.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 12.7% over five sessions with another 4% leg lower today, and there's no dated company catalyst on the board to anchor a window — next earnings isn't scheduled here and triple witching in September is too far out and too generic to lean on. The tape is doing the work without a fundamental trigger, which usually means waiting for either a stabilization signal or a confirmed earnings date before committing fresh capital. Until a dated print is on the calendar, this is a price-action read, not an event read.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (no dated company catalyst currently on the board)
ORCL.bull: OCI/AI backlog story remains the thesis; a 12%+ pullback into a confirmed earnings date could set up a clean add window.
ORCL.bear: No imminent dated catalyst to mark a bottom; momentum break can extend, and the next earnings print is the real test of the AI revenue ramp.

NOK.read: NOK is down 4.6% today and 7% on the week with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window — so there's nothing specific to lean into here. The selloff is happening in a vacuum, which makes it hard to size up without knowing if it's tape-driven or a leak ahead of guidance. Until a Q3 print date gets confirmed or a network/AI deal headline lands, this is a price-action watch, not an event trade.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no NOK-specific dated catalyst on calendar)
NOK.bull: Quality telco infra name pulling back on no news; reclaim of recent range would suggest the flush is technical.
NOK.bear: A 7% five-day drop with no catalyst often front-runs bad news — guide-down or contract loss risk before the next print.

NBIS.read: No dated company catalyst on the calendar for NBIS — no confirmed or estimated earnings, no ex-div — so there's no event-defined window to lean into here. The only thing ahead is Sep 18 triple witching, which is a market-wide vol event, not an NBIS-specific catalyst. Until an earnings date or company-specific event prints, this is a price-action name, not a catalyst name.
NBIS.bull: AI infra demand and GPU-cloud narrative keep bid under the name absent any negative print.
NBIS.bear: No dated catalyst means no support floor — drifts on sentiment and headline risk into Sep opex.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position with no dated company catalysts on the board — next earnings isn't even on the calendar yet, and the stock is doing nothing notable (down 1.2% on the week, up half a percent today). With no print to lean into and only a distant triple witching as macro noise, there's no edge to acting here. The window to revisit opens when KO's next earnings date and ex-div date get confirmed.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific catalyst on the board)
KO.bull: Defensive staple holding its range; quiet tape with no binary risk near-term means dividend compounding does the work.
KO.bear: No dated catalyst means no scheduled re-rating mechanism — capital is parked, not working, and FX/volume soft patches can leak in without warning.

SPCX.read: Tiny 10-share position in a freshly public name (June 2026 IPO) with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates yet — coverage gaps are normal this early. The only dated item on the horizon is Sep 18 triple witching, which can amplify volatility in low-float recent IPOs but isn't a fundamental catalyst. Without a confirmed first post-IPO print to anchor a window, this is a watch until SpaceX schedules its inaugural earnings call or lockup details surface.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro vol event; no SPCX-specific catalyst dated yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity premium in the first true SpaceX-linked public vehicle keeps a bid under it through the post-IPO discovery phase.
SPCX.bear: Recent-IPO supply/lockup dynamics plus Sep triple-witching vol can drive a sharp air-pocket with no earnings floor to catch it.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
